notes: Marked applicable:false for the claudecode adapter — see recipe by_adapter.claudecode.applicable. Triggering a controlled mid-turn API error from claudecode requires network manipulation (pfctl block, closed-port ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) or quota exhaustion; the current driver has no deterministic mechanism. Per the assessment (irrlicht_observes: partial, confidence 0.55) failures split into three buckets: Bucket A (transcript epilogue with synthesized error text → working→ready via classifier rule 2b, observable), Bucket B (pure mid-stream network failure with NO transcript write → no inactivity sweep exists today, can leave session stuck in working — this is the spec gap), Bucket C (Claude Code emits system/turn_duration on abnormal exit → turn_done fires, observable). The phases below are the SHAPE future work would assert against Buckets A/C — they are NOT runnable today and are recorded here as documentation only.

text: FUTURE ASSERTION: session appears in ready
FUTURE ASSERTION: user prompt drives ready → working before the API call fails
FUTURE ASSERTION: error epilogue OR system/turn_duration drives working → ready within sweep interval; state does not stick in working indefinitely
